matches.match_rationale: Assessment rubric divergence is fundamentally a documentation problem — each campus has informal or inconsistent grading standards that have never been codified into a shared, authoritative set of rubrics. Text generation can draft network-wide grading rubrics, standardized assessment frameworks, and pass/fail criteria for each subject, tailored to the operator's curriculum scope. This directly addresses the absence of written standards that causes inter-campus grading gaps.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI tool that ingests existing campus rubrics and assessment documents and generates a canonical, harmonized rubric set for each subject — with explicit pass thresholds, grade descriptors, and annotator guidance — ready for curriculum director review and rollout.

matches.match_rationale: Each campus likely maintains grading data in its own format — different SIS exports, spreadsheet schemas, or grade-book structures. Normalizing these into a single comparable schema is a prerequisite for detecting divergence, benchmarking pass rates, and producing comparable transcripts. AI-assisted schema mapping handles the messy reality of per-campus data heterogeneity without requiring a full SIS replacement.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A data pipeline that ingests multi-campus grade exports in varying formats, maps them to a unified grading schema, and outputs a normalized dataset that powers cross-campus comparability reporting and transcript standardization.

matches.match_rationale: The core symptom — a 70% pass rate at one campus vs 45% at another for the same subject — is exactly the kind of statistical outlier anomaly detection is designed to surface. AI can flag campuses or individual assessors whose grade distributions deviate significantly from the network norm, prompting targeted review rather than waiting for an accreditor or employer to notice. This turns reactive discovery into a proactive governance signal.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A grading-consistency monitor that continuously compares grade distributions across campuses per subject and cohort, flagging statistical outliers to the academic standards team as potential calibration issues requiring review.

matches.match_rationale: Curriculum directors and campus principals need to query the network's assessment standards — 'What is the approved rubric for Year 10 Mathematics extended response?' — and get a grounded, citable answer rather than hunting across SharePoint folders. A RAG system over the canonical rubric library ensures every assessor is working from the same source of truth, with answers generated from the authoritative document corpus. This directly reduces the inconsistency caused by staff not knowing or finding the standard.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal standards Q&A assistant that lets any teacher or campus leader query the network's assessment policy library and receive cited, accurate answers about approved rubrics, grade thresholds, and moderation procedures.

matches.match_rationale: Grade moderation — the process of reviewing a sample of student work across campuses to verify consistent application of rubrics — is a multi-step workflow: pulling samples, routing to moderators, collecting feedback, escalating discrepancies, and updating records. An automation agent can orchestrate this moderation pipeline across campuses on a scheduled or triggered basis, replacing the ad-hoc email chains and spreadsheet trackers that currently allow divergence to go undetected. This addresses the governance gap, not just the symptom.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A moderation workflow agent that automatically samples student assessments from each campus each term, routes them to cross-campus moderators, collects calibration verdicts, and generates a moderation report with flagged discrepancies for the academic standards committee.
